steps.title: Identify the tense pattern
steps.content: The sentence uses two present tense verbs: atoms travel faster and faster, and they are diverted to collide with a target. The blank sits inside a temporal “until” clause and must be consistent with this present-tense pattern describing a recurring process.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — will reach: Future tense. Violates tense consistency — mixing future tense with the present tense verbs travel and are diverted creates a logical inconsistency. ✗
  • Choice B — reach: Simple present tense. Matches travel and are diverted, correctly describing the recurring condition atoms must meet. ✓
  • Choice C — had reached: Past perfect tense. Violates tense consistency — shifts the clause into the past, inconsistent with the present tense context. ✗
  • Choice D — are reaching: Present progressive tense. Violates tense consistency — suggests an action currently in progress rather than a general recurring condition, inconsistent with the simple present verbs in the sentence. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
